---
title: "How to serial interval?"
author: "Sam Abbott"
institute: "London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-notes: ""
date: 2026-06-16
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"
---

## Talk plan

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="68%"}
- What is a serial interval, and why do we want it?
- A delay seen through censoring and truncation
- The same machinery in software, and beyond transmission pairs
- A within-host informed approach, and a generic alternative
- Delay estimation in practice
- Representing composed distributions
- How can you help me?
:::
::: {.column width="32%"}
![](figures/fig-qr.png){width="58%"}

::: {.attribution}
[samabbott.co.uk/how-to-serial-interval](https://samabbott.co.uk/how-to-serial-interval)

Lorentz Center workshop, *Connecting Survival Analysis and Infectious
Disease Modeling*, Leiden, 16 June 2026.
:::
:::
::::

{{< include _partials/01-intro.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/02-censoring.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/03-extending.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/04-paper.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/05-practice.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/06-composed.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/07-collaboration.qmd >}}

# Wrapping up

## Summary

- A serial interval is a delay seen through censoring and truncation:
  within-interval censoring of both events, and right truncation for
  dynamical bias
- Treated simply it is event-to-event; really it is a **composition**
  of delays, which is where the biology, or a generic hazard, enters
- Within-host informed models couple the delays; generic
  contact-interval models need less
- The estimate is only as good as the data collection and the biases
  behind it
- Composable tooling lets us write these delays once and reuse them
  across model types

## Thank you

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="62%"}
An ecosystem for estimating and using delays:

- [epidist](https://epidist.epinowcast.org/) — individual-level delay
  estimation
- [primarycensored](https://primarycensored.epinowcast.org/) —
  censored, truncated delays
- [CensoredDistributions.jl](https://github.com/EpiAware/CensoredDistributions.jl)
  — composing delays
- [EpiNow2](https://epiforecasts.io/EpiNow2/) and
  [epinowcast](https://package.epinowcast.org/) — $R_t$ and nowcasting
- [EpiBranch.jl](https://github.com/epiforecasts/EpiBranch.jl) —
  branching-process models
:::
::: {.column width="38%"}
![](figures/fig-qr.png){width="62%"}

::: {.attribution}
[samabbott.co.uk/how-to-serial-interval](https://samabbott.co.uk/how-to-serial-interval)
:::
:::
::::
